response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering fundamentals with 19 years of experience and proven architectural capabilities, there is a critical skill mismatch. The role requires a C++ specialist with recent hands-on expertise in systems programming, industrial protocols, and embedded/on-premises solutions. The candidate's last significant C++ work was over 10 years ago; the past decade has been focused on web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React). Additionally, there is no demonstrated background in industrial automation, IoT, message brokers, MQTT, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), which are core to the role.
strengths: Extensive architecture and system design experience across multiple platforms and domains; demonstrates strong grasp of software organization and scalability principles.
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing capability through workshops, talks, and established testing/quality culture; aligns with the behavioral requirement for mentoring team members.
Highly adaptable full-stack engineer with 19 years of experience and ability to pick appropriate technologies; shows strong analytical and problem-solving mindset.
concerns: C++ expertise is severely dated (last significant work ~2015, now 10+ years ago); primary focus for past decade has been TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js, not systems programming.
Zero demonstrated experience in industrial automation, IoT, manufacturing, or embedded systems; background is web applications, GIS platforms, and fintech—entirely different domain.

Missing key technical competencies required for the role: no mention of MQTT, message brokers, multi-threading architectures, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 secure development standards.
